extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won from a breakaway after surviving 192km in extreme heat, out-sprinting WRIGHT Fred and HOULE Hugo in Saint-Étienne.
tactical_patterns: SAGAN Peter actively chased breakaway inclusion but other specialists rejected cooperation knowing they'd lose the sprint
Breakaway established only after categorized climb inflicted damage, suggesting GC teams welcomed active break formation to reduce later attacks
PEDERSEN Mads forced HOULE Hugo into leadout duties despite Houle's reluctance, exploiting positional dominance before finale
VAN AERT Wout sprinted for intermediate points with LAPORTE Christophe leadout despite controlling green jersey, signaling total dominance

rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: former world champion showed superior tactical positioning by forcing Houle to lead despite being strongest sprinter
rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: negative: rejected by breakaway specialists who understood sprint futility against him, revealing reputation prevents collaboration
rider_signals.WRIGHT Fred: neutral: unknown sprint capacity revealed in finale but insufficient against world-class closer Pedersen
rider_signals.HOULE Hugo: neutral: reluctant leadout work suggests unfamiliarity with stage-winning tactics despite making decisive break
rider_signals.VAN AERT Wout: positive: contested intermediate sprint with full leadout despite commanding green jersey lead, projecting dominance
rider_signals.JORGENSON Matteo: neutral: bridged from peloton but arrived too late for podium contention
rider_signals.VINGEGAARD Jonas: neutral: survived third consecutive stage without attacks from Pogačar, maintaining 2:22 yellow jersey advantage
